title: dicliptera cochabambensis
intro: Dicliptera cochabambensis, also known as the Bolivian snapwort, is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects. Dicliptera cochabambensis can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and stems can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
